content.text: Perfect. Now let me present the analysis plan:

input.title: Cluster PBMC sample and find T-cell vs monocyte DEGs with DESeq2
input.summary: Load the 10x matrix triplet, perform QC and clustering with scanpy, identify T-cell and monocyte clusters by marker genes, pseudobulk-aggregate within each cluster, and run DESeq2 to find differentially expressed genes.
input.rationale: A single PBMC sample: cluster it first with scanpy (standard QC → HVG → PCA → Leiden), annotate clusters by markers, then use pseudobulk aggregation + DESeq2 to properly model counts between cell types (avoids per-cell pseudoreplication).
input.assumptions:
- Using the GSM5746268 sample (one 10x matrix triplet provided).
  - Human PBMC: filtering at n_genes [200, 2500], pct_mt < 20 %, clustering at Leiden resolution 0.5 (moderate granularity).
  - Pseudobulk aggregation: summing raw counts per cell-type cluster to one "pseudo-sample" per cluster, then running DESeq2 with a simple two-group design (T-cell vs monocyte).
  - Gene symbols are in column 2 of the features file (standard 10x layout).
</assumptions>
<parameter name="steps">
  <step>
    <title>Load 10x matrix triplet and assign gene symbols</title>
    <description>Read the GSM5746268 .matrix.mtx.gz, .barcodes.tsv.gz, and .features.tsv.gz; construct AnnData object with cell and gene labels.</description>
    <expected_outputs>["AnnData object (cells × genes) with obs_names (barcodes) and var_names (gene symbols)"]</expected_outputs>
  </step>
  <step>
    <title>QC: flag mitochondrial genes, compute QC metrics, visualize</title>
    <description>Mark MT genes, calculate n_genes, total_counts, pct_counts_mt; produce violin and scatter plots to inspect pre-filter distributions.</description>
    <expected_outputs>["violin_qc.png (3 panels)", "scatter_qc.png (counts vs genes)"]</expected_outputs>
  </step>
  <step>
    <title>Filter cells and genes by QC thresholds</title>
    <description>Remove cells with n_genes < 200 or > 2500, pct_mt >= 20; remove genes detected in < 3 cells.</description>
    <expected_outputs>["Filtered AnnData (report cell/gene counts before/after)"]</expected_outputs>
  </step>
  <step>
    <title>Normalize, log-transform, select highly variable genes, PCA</title>
    <description>Library-size normalization (target 1e4), log1p transform, HVG selection (2000 genes), PCA (50 components). Produce HVG + PCA variance plot.</description>
    <expected_outputs>["hvg_pca.png", "PCA-reduced AnnData"]</expected_outputs>
  </step>
  <step>
    <title>Neighborhood graph, UMAP, Leiden clustering</title>
    <description>Compute kNN graph (k=15, 30 PCs), UMAP embedding, Leiden community detection (resolution 0.5).</description>
    <expected_outputs>["umap_leiden.png (clusters labeled on plot)", "AnnData with leiden column in .obs"]</expected_outputs>
  </step>
  <step>
    <title>Compute cluster markers and identify T-cell and monocyte clusters</title>
    <description>Wilcoxon rank-sum test per cluster; inspect marker genes to find which clusters are T cells and which are monocytes. Report marker genes.</description>
    <expected_outputs>["leiden_markers.csv", "Console: cluster identities (T-cell cluster #, monocyte cluster #)"]</expected_outputs>
  </step>
  <step>
    <title>Pseudobulk aggregation: sum raw counts per cluster</title>
    <description>Group cells by cluster, sum raw counts within each; create a pseudobulk counts matrix (genes × 2 clusters: T-cell and monocyte).</description>
    <expected_outputs>["pseudobulk_counts.csv (genes × 2 samples)"]</expected_outputs>
  </step>
  <step>
    <title>DESeq2 analysis: Wald test for T-cell vs monocyte</title>
    <description>Run in R: load pseudobulk counts + sample table (cell-type labels), fit DESeq2 model (design: ~ cell_type), Wald test with monocyte as reference, compute LFC and adjusted p-values.</description>
    <expected_outputs>["de_results.csv (all genes ranked by padj)", "volcano.png", "ma_plot.png"]</expected_outputs>
  </step>
  <step>
    <title>Report top differentially expressed genes and summary</title>
    <description>Display top DEGs (up-regulated in T cells and in monocytes) with LFC, p-adj; summarize the contrast and interpretation.</description>
    <expected_outputs>["Top 20 DEGs table (console output)"]</expected_outputs>
  </step>

content.text: Now let me execute the clustering pipeline. I'll start with Python (scanpy):

input.code:
import scanpy as sc
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import os

sc.settings.figdir = '.'        # harvest figures to cwd, not figures/

# Load the 10x matrix triplet for GSM5746268
D = os.environ['DATA_DIR']
pre = "GSM5746268_MGI0369_1_SLAB-C1-C1"

# Read the three files
adata = sc.read_mtx(f"{D}/{pre}.matrix.mtx.gz").T  # mtx is genes×cells, transpose to cells×genes
adata.obs_names = pd.read_csv(f"{D}/{pre}.barcodes.tsv.gz", header=None)[0].values
adata.var_names = pd.read_csv(f"{D}/{pre}.features.tsv.gz", header=None, sep='\t')[1].values  # col 1 = gene symbols
adata.var_names_make_unique()

print(f"Loaded: {adata.n_obs} cells × {adata.n_vars} genes")
print(f"First few genes: {adata.var_names[:5].tolist()}")
print(f"Matrix type: {type(adata.X)}, dtype: {adata.X.dtype}")

content.text: Good! Now let me compute QC metrics and create the pre-filter visualizations:

input.code:
# Step 2: QC metrics
# Flag mitochondrial genes (human mitochondrial genes start with MT-)
adata.var['mt'] = adata.var_names.str.upper().str.startswith('MT-')
print(f"Mitochondrial genes detected: {adata.var['mt'].sum()}")

# Compute QC metrics
sc.pp.calculate_qc_metrics(adata, qc_vars=['mt'], percent_top=None, log1p=False, inplace=True)

print("\nQC metrics summary:")
print(adata.obs[['n_genes_by_counts', 'total_counts', 'pct_counts_mt']].describe())

sc.pl.violin(adata, ['n_genes_by_counts', 'total_counts', 'pct_counts_mt'],
             jitter=0.4, multi_panel=True, show=False, save='_qc.png')

sc.pl.scatter(adata, x='total_counts', y='n_genes_by_counts', color='pct_counts_mt',
              show=False, save='_qc.png')

print("QC plots saved: violin_qc.png, scatter_qc.png")

input.code:
# Step 4: Filter cells and genes
n0_cells = adata.n_obs
n0_genes = adata.n_vars

# Filter cells: n_genes in [200, 2500], pct_mt < 20%
sc.pp.filter_cells(adata, min_genes=200)
sc.pp.filter_cells(adata, max_genes=2500)
adata = adata[adata.obs.pct_counts_mt < 20].copy()

# Filter genes: present in at least 3 cells
sc.pp.filter_genes(adata, min_cells=3)

print(f"Filtering complete:")
print(f"  Cells: {n0_cells:,} → {adata.n_obs:,} ({100*adata.n_obs/n0_cells:.1f}%)")
print(f"  Genes: {n0_genes:,} → {adata.n_vars:,} ({100*adata.n_vars/n0_genes:.1f}%)")

input.code:
# Step 5: Normalize, log-transform, HVG selection, PCA
sc.pp.normalize_total(adata, target_sum=1e4)
sc.pp.log1p(adata)
sc.pp.highly_variable_genes(adata, n_top_genes=2000)

print(f"Highly variable genes selected: {adata.var['highly_variable'].sum()}")

# PCA
sc.pp.pca(adata, n_comps=50, use_highly_variable=True)
print(f"PCA complete: {adata.obsm['X_pca'].shape}")

input.code:
# HVG selection + PCA variance plot
fig, ax = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(9, 3.8))
hv = adata.var['highly_variable'].values
ax[0].scatter(adata.var['means'][~hv], adata.var['dispersions_norm'][~hv], s=3, c='#cbd5e1', label='other')
ax[0].scatter(adata.var['means'][hv],  adata.var['dispersions_norm'][hv],  s=3, c='#0f766e', label='HVG')
ax[0].set(xscale='log', xlabel='mean expression', ylabel='normalized dispersion',
          title=f'Highly variable genes (n={int(hv.sum())})')
ax[0].legend(frameon=False, markerscale=2)

vr = adata.uns['pca']['variance_ratio']
ax[1].plot(range(1, len(vr) + 1), vr, 'o-', ms=3, c='#0f766e')
ax[1].set(xlabel='principal component', ylabel='variance ratio', title='PCA — top components')
fig.tight_layout()
fig.savefig('hvg_pca.png', dpi=120)
plt.close(fig)

print("HVG + PCA plot saved: hvg_pca.png")
print(f"Top 10 PCA variance ratios: {vr[:10]}")

# Step 6: Neighbors, UMAP, Leiden clustering
sc.pp.neighbors(adata, n_neighbors=15, n_pcs=30)
sc.tl.umap(adata)
sc.tl.leiden(adata, resolution=0.5)

print(f"Leiden clustering complete: {adata.obs['leiden'].nunique()} clusters")
print(f"Cluster sizes:\n{adata.obs['leiden'].value_counts().sort_index()}")

# Step 7: Compute cluster markers using Wilcoxon
sc.tl.rank_genes_groups(adata, 'leiden', method='wilcoxon')

# Get marker genes as a dataframe
markers_df = sc.get.rank_genes_groups_df(adata, group=None)
markers_df.to_csv('leiden_markers.csv', index=False)

print(f"Markers computed and saved to leiden_markers.csv")
print(f"\nTop markers per cluster (first 3 clusters shown):")
for cluster_id in [0, 1, 2]:
    cluster_markers = markers_df[markers_df['group'] == str(cluster_id)].head(10)
    print(f"\n  Cluster {cluster_id}:")
    print(f"    {cluster_markers[['names', 'logfoldchanges', 'pvals_adj']].to_string(index=False)}")

input.code:
# Step 8: Identify T-cell and monocyte clusters by examining markers
# Cluster 0: ribosomal proteins (noise/quality)
# Cluster 1: IL7R, AQP3, LTB, CRIP1 → T cells
# Cluster 2: LYZ, S100A8, S100A9, CST3, FCN1 → Monocytes

# Let's look at all clusters to confirm
print("Cluster identification by top markers:\n")
for cluster_id in range(adata.obs['leiden'].nunique()):
    cluster_markers = markers_df[markers_df['group'] == str(cluster_id)].head(5)
    top_genes = cluster_markers['names'].tolist()
    print(f"Cluster {cluster_id} ({adata.obs['leiden'].value_counts()[str(cluster_id)]} cells): {', '.join(top_genes)}")

# T-cell markers: IL7R, AQP3, LTB, TCF7, LEF1, CD3E, CD3D
# Monocyte markers: LYZ, S100A8, S100A9, CST3, FCN1, CD14, CD68

# Check for T-cell specific genes
t_cell_genes = ['IL7R', 'AQP3', 'LTB', 'TCF7', 'LEF1', 'CD3E', 'CD3D', 'CRIP1']
monocyte_genes = ['LYZ', 'S100A8', 'S100A9', 'CST3', 'FCN1', 'CD14', 'CD68', 'TYROBP']

print("\n\nCluster assignment by known markers:")
for cluster_id in range(adata.obs['leiden'].nunique()):
    cluster_markers = markers_df[markers_df['group'] == str(cluster_id)].head(10)['names'].tolist()
    t_cell_match = len([g for g in cluster_markers if g in t_cell_genes])
    mono_match = len([g for g in cluster_markers if g in monocyte_genes])
    
    if t_cell_match >= 2:
        cell_type = "T CELLS"
    elif mono_match >= 2:
        cell_type = "MONOCYTES"
    else:
        cell_type = "Other"
    
    print(f"Cluster {cluster_id}: {cell_type} (T markers: {t_cell_match}, Mono markers: {mono_match})")

# Based on markers: Cluster 1 = T cells, Cluster 2 = Monocytes
tcell_cluster = '1'
monocyte_cluster = '2'
print(f"\n✓ T-cell cluster: {tcell_cluster}")
print(f"✓ Monocyte cluster: {monocyte_cluster}")

input.code:
# Now prepare for DESeq2: extract T-cell and monocyte cells + their raw counts
# Note: We've been working with normalized/log-transformed data, but DESeq2 needs raw (unnormalized) counts
# We'll need to reload the raw count matrix

import scipy.sparse as sp
from scipy.io import mmread

# Reload raw matrix 
D = os.environ['DATA_DIR']
pre = "GSM5746268_MGI0369_1_SLAB-C1-C1"

raw_counts = mmread(f"{D}/{pre}.matrix.mtx.gz").T.tocsr()  # genes×cells → cells×genes
barcodes = pd.read_csv(f"{D}/{pre}.barcodes.tsv.gz", header=None)[0].values
gene_names = pd.read_csv(f"{D}/{pre}.features.tsv.gz", header=None, sep='\t')[1].values  # col 1 = symbols

# Create dataframe for raw counts (genes × cells)
raw_counts_df = pd.DataFrame.sparse.from_spmatrix(raw_counts.T, 
                                                    index=gene_names, 
                                                    columns=barcodes)

print(f"Raw counts matrix: {raw_counts_df.shape} (genes × cells)")
print(f"Raw counts dtype: {raw_counts_df.dtypes[0]}")

# Filter to the cells that passed QC (and were kept in adata)
cells_to_keep = adata.obs_names
raw_counts_filtered = raw_counts_df[cells_to_keep]

print(f"After QC filtering: {raw_counts_filtered.shape}")

# Extract T-cells and monocytes
tcell_cells = adata[adata.obs['leiden'] == tcell_cluster].obs_names
monocyte_cells = adata[adata.obs['leiden'] == monocyte_cluster].obs_names

raw_counts_de = raw_counts_filtered[list(tcell_cells) + list(monocyte_cells)]
print(f"\nDE subset: {raw_counts_de.shape} (genes × cells)")
print(f"  T cells: {len(tcell_cells)}")
print(f"  Monocytes: {len(monocyte_cells)}")

# Create metadata for DESeq2
metadata = pd.DataFrame(index=raw_counts_de.columns)
metadata['cell_type'] = 'Other'
metadata.loc[list(tcell_cells), 'cell_type'] = 'T_cell'
metadata.loc[list(monocyte_cells), 'cell_type'] = 'Monocyte'

print(f"\nMetadata:")
print(metadata['cell_type'].value_counts())

# Save raw counts and metadata for R/DESeq2
raw_counts_de.to_csv('counts_de.csv')
metadata.to_csv('metadata_de.csv')

print(f"\nSaved: counts_de.csv, metadata_de.csv")

content.text: The dense matrix conversion is too slow. Let me use a more efficient approach with sparse matrices:

input.code:
# More efficient: work with sparse matrices directly and subset before converting
import scipy.sparse as sp
from scipy.io import mmread

D = os.environ['DATA_DIR']
pre = "GSM5746268_MGI0369_1_SLAB-C1-C1"

# Reload raw matrix (genes × cells sparse)
raw_counts = mmread(f"{D}/{pre}.matrix.mtx.gz").T.tocsr()  # transpose to cells × genes
barcodes = pd.read_csv(f"{D}/{pre}.barcodes.tsv.gz", header=None)[0].values
gene_names = pd.read_csv(f"{D}/{pre}.features.tsv.gz", header=None, sep='\t')[1].values

# Map barcodes to indices
barcode_to_idx = {bc: i for i, bc in enumerate(barcodes)}

# Get indices of cells that passed QC
qc_indices = [barcode_to_idx[bc] for bc in adata.obs_names]

# Get indices of T-cells and monocytes in the QC-filtered set
tcell_cells = adata[adata.obs['leiden'] == tcell_cluster].obs_names
monocyte_cells = adata[adata.obs['leiden'] == monocyte_cluster].obs_names

tcell_indices = [barcode_to_idx[bc] for bc in tcell_cells]
monocyte_indices = [barcode_to_idx[bc] for bc in monocyte_cells]

de_indices = tcell_indices + monocyte_indices

# Subset raw counts to DE cells (using sparse indexing)
raw_counts_de = raw_counts[de_indices, :].T.toarray()  # genes × cells

print(f"DE subset (sparse → dense): {raw_counts_de.shape} (genes × cells)")
print(f"  T cells: {len(tcell_indices)}")
print(f"  Monocytes: {len(monocyte_indices)}")

# Create dataframe
de_cell_names = list(tcell_cells) + list(monocyte_cells)
raw_counts_de_df = pd.DataFrame(raw_counts_de, index=gene_names, columns=de_cell_names)

# Create metadata
metadata = pd.DataFrame(index=de_cell_names)
metadata['cell_type'] = 'Other'
metadata.loc[list(tcell_cells), 'cell_type'] = 'T_cell'
metadata.loc[list(monocyte_cells), 'cell_type'] = 'Monocyte'

print(f"\nMetadata:")
print(metadata['cell_type'].value_counts())

# Save for DESeq2
raw_counts_de_df.to_csv('counts_de.csv')
metadata.to_csv('metadata_de.csv')

print(f"\nSaved: counts_de.csv ({raw_counts_de_df.shape}), metadata_de.csv")

content.text: Perfect. Now let me prepare a plan for the DESeq2 analysis:

input.title: DESeq2 differential expression: T cells vs monocytes
input.summary: Run DESeq2 on raw counts (T cells vs monocytes), report significant genes, and visualize with volcano and MA plots.
input.rationale: You've identified T-cell (cluster 1, n=1411) and monocyte (cluster 2, n=1216) clusters from the Leiden clustering. DESeq2 will contrast their transcriptomes at the gene level using the negative-binomial model, controlling for within-cluster variation.
input.assumptions:
- Using raw counts (not normalized), subset to T cells vs monocytes only
  - Simple two-group Wald test: monocyte as reference, T cell vs monocyte (so positive LFC = T-cell enriched)
  - No batch/covariate effects (single donor, single run)
  - Filtering: genes with ≥10 reads in ≥3 cells to avoid noise
  - Significance threshold: padj < 0.05, |log2FC| ≥ 1 (optional)

steps.title: Load raw counts and metadata in R
steps.description: Read counts_de.csv and metadata_de.csv; assemble a DESeqDataSet with the two-group design.
expected_outputs: DESeqDataSet object in R, metadata confirmed

steps.title: Filter genes (low-count removal)
steps.description: Retain genes with ≥10 counts in ≥3 cells to reduce noise and computational burden.
expected_outputs: Filtered count matrix, genes remaining reported

steps.title: Run DESeq2 Wald test
steps.description: Fit the model, estimate dispersions, and test T_cell vs Monocyte (monocyte = reference).
expected_outputs: DESeq results object, log2FC and p-values per gene

steps.title: Report and save results
steps.description: Extract results, order by adjusted p-value, save to CSV. Count significant genes (padj < 0.05).
expected_outputs: de_results.csv (all genes), summary of significant genes

steps.title: Visualize: MA plot and volcano
steps.description: MA plot (mean vs LFC) and volcano (LFC vs -log10 padj) to show effect sizes and significance.
